Studies and Reports, , , provided that:Pub. L. 105–33, title IV, § 4705(c)111 Stat. 500Aug. 5, 1997“(1)GAO study and report on quality assurance and accreditation standards.—“(A).—StudyThe Comptroller General of the United States shall conduct a study and analysis of the quality assurance programs and accreditation standards applicable to managed care entities operating in the private sector, or to such entities that operate under contracts under the medicare program under title XVIII of the Social Security Act ( et seq.). Such study shall determine—42 U.S.C. 1395“(i)if such programs and standards include consideration of the accessibility and quality of the health care items and services delivered under such contracts to low-income individuals; and“(ii)the appropriateness of applying such programs and standards to medicaid managed care organizations under section 1932(c) of such Act [].42 U.S.C. 1396u–2(c)“(B).—ReportThe Comptroller General shall submit a report to the Committee on Commerce [now Committee on Energy and Commerce] of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Finance of the Senate on the study conducted under subparagraph (A).“(2)Study and report on services provided to individuals with special health care needs.—“(A).—StudyThe Secretary of Health and Human Services, in consultation with States, managed care organizations, the National Academy of State Health Policy, representatives of beneficiaries with special health care needs, experts in specialized health care, and others, shall conduct a study concerning safeguards (if any) that may be needed to ensure that the health care needs of individuals with special health care needs and chronic conditions who are enrolled with medicaid managed care organizations are adequately met.“(B).—ReportNot later than 2 years after the date of the enactment of this Act [], the Secretary shall submit to Committees described in paragraph (1)(B) a report on such study.”Aug. 5, 1997

children.heading: State option to use managed care
children.label: a State option to use managed care

children.heading: Use of medicaid managed care organizations and primary care case managers
children.label: 1 Use of medicaid managed care organizations and primary care case managers
I
children.text: the entity and the contract with the State meet the applicable requirements of this section and or , andsection 1396b(m) of this titlesection 1396d(t) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: the requirements described in the succeeding paragraphs of this subsection are met; and

children.label: ii
children.text: may restrict the number of provider agreements with managed care entities under the State plan if such restriction does not substantially impair access to services.

children.label: i
children.text: a medicaid managed care organization, as defined in , that provides or arranges for services for enrollees under a contract pursuant to ; andsection 1396b(m)(1)(A) of this titlesection 1396b(m) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: a primary care case manager, as defined in .section 1396d(t)(2) of this title

children.heading: Exemption of certain children with special needs
children.label: A Exemption of certain children with special needs
i
children.text: is eligible for supplemental security income under subchapter XVI;

children.label: ii
children.text: is described in ;section 701(a)(1)(D) of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: is described in ;section 1396a(e)(3) of this title

children.label: iv
children.text: is receiving foster care or adoption assistance under part E of subchapter IV; or

children.label: v
children.text: is in foster care or otherwise in an out-of-home placement.

children.heading: Exemption of medicare beneficiaries
children.label: B Exemption of medicare beneficiaries
children.text: A State may not require under paragraph (1) the enrollment in a managed care entity of an individual who is a qualified medicare beneficiary (as defined in ) or an individual otherwise eligible for benefits under subchapter XVIII.section 1396d(p)(1) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: The Indian Health Service.

children.label: ii
children.text: An Indian health program operated by an Indian tribe or tribal organization pursuant to a contract, grant, cooperative agreement, or compact with the Indian Health Service pursuant to the Indian Self-Determination Act [ et seq.].25 U.S.C. 5321

children.label: iii
children.text: An urban Indian health program operated by an urban Indian organization pursuant to a grant or contract with the Indian Health Service pursuant to title V of the Indian Health Care Improvement Act [ et seq.].25 U.S.C. 1651

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A State must permit an individual to choose a managed care entity from not less than two such entities that meet the applicable requirements of this section, and of or .section 1396b(m) of this titlesection 1396d(t) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: permits the individual to receive such assistance through not less than two physicians or case managers (to the extent that at least two physicians or case managers are available to provide such assistance in the area), and

children.label: ii
children.text: permits the individual to obtain such assistance from any other provider in appropriate circumstances (as established by the State under regulations of the Secretary).

children.heading: Treatment of certain county-operated health insuring organizations
children.label: C Treatment of certain county-operated health insuring organizations
I
children.text: first became operational prior to , orJanuary 1, 1986

children.label: II
children.text: is described in section 9517(c)(3) of the Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act of 1985 (as added by section 4734(2) of the Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act of 1990), and

children.label: ii
children.text: the individual is given a choice between at least two providers within such entity.

children.heading: Process for enrollment and termination and change of enrollment
children.label: 4 Process for enrollment and termination and change of enrollment
i
children.text: for cause at any time (consistent with ), andsection 1396b(m)(2)(A)(vi) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: during the 90-day period beginning on the date the individual receives notice of such enrollment, and

children.label: II
children.text: at least every 12 months thereafter.

children.heading: Notice of termination rights
children.label: B Notice of termination rights
children.text: The State shall provide for notice to each such individual of the opportunity to terminate (or change) enrollment under such conditions. Such notice shall be provided at least 60 days before each annual enrollment opportunity described in subparagraph (A)(ii)(II).

children.heading: Enrollment priorities
children.label: C Enrollment priorities
children.text: In carrying out paragraph (1)(A), the State shall establish a method for establishing enrollment priorities in the case of a managed care entity that does not have sufficient capacity to enroll all such individuals seeking enrollment under which individuals already enrolled with the entity are given priority in continuing enrollment with the entity.

children.label: i
children.text: under which any such individual who does not enroll with a managed care entity during the enrollment period specified by the State shall be enrolled by the State with such an entity which has not been found to be out of substantial compliance with the applicable requirements of this section and of or ; andsection 1396b(m) of this titlesection 1396d(t) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: maintaining existing provider-individual relationships or relationships with providers that have traditionally served beneficiaries under this subchapter; and

children.label: II
children.text: if maintaining such provider relationships is not possible, the equitable distribution of such individuals among qualified managed care entities available to enroll such individuals, consistent with the enrollment capacities of the entities.

children.heading: Information in easily understood form
children.label: A Information in easily understood form
children.text: Each State, enrollment broker, or managed care entity shall provide all enrollment notices and informational and instructional materials relating to such an entity under this subchapter in a manner and form which may be easily understood by enrollees and potential enrollees of the entity who are eligible for medical assistance under the State plan under this subchapter.

children.heading: Information to enrollees and potential enrollees
children.label: B Information to enrollees and potential enrollees

children.heading: Providers
children.label: i Providers
children.text: The identity, locations, qualifications, and availability of health care providers that participate with the organization, including as required by subparagraph (E).

children.heading: Enrollee rights and responsibilities
children.label: ii Enrollee rights and responsibilities
children.text: The rights and responsibilities of enrollees.

children.heading: Grievance and appeal procedures
children.label: iii Grievance and appeal procedures
children.text: The procedures available to an enrollee and a health care provider to challenge or appeal the failure of the organization to cover a service.

children.heading: Information on covered items and services
children.label: iv Information on covered items and services
children.text: All items and services that are available to enrollees under the contract between the State and the organization that are covered either directly or through a method of referral and prior authorization. Each managed care entity that is a primary care case manager shall, upon request, make available to enrollees and potential enrollees in the organization’s service area the information described in clause (iii).

children.heading: Benefits and cost-sharing
children.label: i Benefits and cost-sharing
children.text: The benefits covered and cost-sharing imposed by the entity.

children.heading: Service area
children.label: ii Service area
children.text: The service area of the entity.

children.heading: Quality and performance
children.label: iii Quality and performance
children.text: To the extent available, quality and performance indicators for the benefits under the entity.

children.heading: Information on benefits not covered under managed care arrangement
children.label: D Information on benefits not covered under managed care arrangement
children.text: A State, directly or through managed care entities, shall, on or before an individual enrolls with such an entity under this subchapter, inform the enrollee in a written and prominent manner of any benefits to which the enrollee may be entitled to under this subchapter but which are not made available to the enrollee through the entity. Such information shall include information on where and how such enrollees may access benefits not made available to the enrollee through the entity.

children.label: I
children.text: the name of the provider;

children.label: II
children.text: the specialty of the provider;

children.label: III
children.text: the address at which the provider provides services;

children.label: IV
children.text: the telephone number of the provider; and

children.label: aa
children.text: the provider’s cultural and linguistic capabilities, including languages (including American Sign Language) offered by the provider or by a skilled medical interpreter who provides interpretation services at the provider’s office;

children.label: bb
children.text: whether the provider is accepting as new patients, individuals who receive medical assistance under this subchapter;

children.label: cc
children.text: whether the provider’s office or facility has accommodations for individuals with physical disabilities, including offices, exam rooms, and equipment;

children.label: dd
children.text: the Internet website of such provider, if applicable; and

children.label: ee
children.text: whether the provider offers covered services via telehealth; and

children.label: VI
children.text: other relevant information, as required by the Secretary.

children.heading: Network provider defined
children.label: ii Network provider defined
children.text: In this subparagraph, the term “network provider” includes any provider, group of providers, or entity that has a network provider agreement with a managed care organization, a prepaid inpatient health plan (as defined by the Secretary), a prepaid ambulatory health plan (as defined by the Secretary), or a primary care case management entity (as defined by the Secretary) or a subcontractor of any such entity or plan, and receives payment under this subchapter directly or indirectly to order, refer, or render covered services as a result of the State’s contract with the entity or plan. For purposes of this subparagraph, a network provider shall not be considered to be a subcontractor by virtue of the network provider agreement.

children.heading: Specification of benefits
children.label: 1 Specification of benefits
children.text: Each contract with a managed care entity under or under shall specify the benefits the provision (or arrangement) for which the entity is responsible.section 1396b(m) of this titlesection 1396d(t)(3) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: to provide coverage for emergency services (as defined in subparagraph (B)) without regard to prior authorization or the emergency care provider’s contractual relationship with the organization or manager, and

children.label: ii
children.text: to comply with guidelines established under (respecting coordination of post-stabilization care) in the same manner as such guidelines apply to Medicare+Choice plans offered under part C of subchapter XVIII.section 1395w–22(d)(2) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: are furnished by a provider that is qualified to furnish such services under this subchapter, and

children.label: ii
children.text: are needed to evaluate or stabilize an emergency medical condition (as defined in subparagraph (C)).

children.label: i
children.text: placing the health of the individual (or, with respect to a pregnant woman, the health of the woman or her unborn child) in serious jeopardy,

children.label: ii
children.text: serious impairment to bodily functions, or

children.label: iii
children.text: serious dysfunction of any bodily organ or part.

children.heading: Emergency services furnished by non-contract providers
children.label: D Emergency services furnished by non-contract providers
children.text: Any provider of emergency services that does not have in effect a contract with a Medicaid managed care entity that establishes payment amounts for services furnished to a beneficiary enrolled in the entity’s Medicaid managed care plan must accept as payment in full no more than the amounts (less any payments for indirect costs of medical education and direct costs of graduate medical education) that it could collect if the beneficiary received medical assistance under this subchapter other than through enrollment in such an entity. In a State where rates paid to hospitals under the State plan are negotiated by contract and not publicly released, the payment amount applicable under this subparagraph shall be the average contract rate that would apply under the State plan for general acute care hospitals or the average contract rate that would apply under such plan for tertiary hospitals.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraphs (B) and (C), under a contract under a medicaid managed care organization (in relation to an individual enrolled under the contract) shall not prohibit or otherwise restrict a covered health care professional (as defined in subparagraph (D)) from advising such an individual who is a patient of the professional about the health status of the individual or medical care or treatment for the individual’s condition or disease, regardless of whether benefits for such care or treatment are provided under the contract, if the professional is acting within the lawful scope of practice.section 1396b(m) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: objects to the provision of such service on moral or religious grounds; and

children.label: ii
children.text: in the manner and through the written instrumentalities such organization deems appropriate, makes available information on its policies regarding such service to prospective enrollees before or during enrollment and to enrollees within 90 days after the date that the organization adopts a change in policy regarding such a counseling or referral service.

children.heading: “Health care professional” defined
children.label: C “Health care professional” defined
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “health care professional” means a physician (as defined in ) or other health care professional if coverage for the professional’s services is provided under the contract referred to in subparagraph (A) for the services of the professional. Such term includes a podiatrist, optometrist, chiropractor, psychologist, dentist, physician assistant, physical or occupational therapist and therapy assistant, speech-language pathologist, audiologist, registered or licensed practical nurse (including nurse practitioner, clinical nurse specialist, certified registered nurse anesthetist, and certified nurse-midwife), licensed certified social worker, registered respiratory therapist, and certified respiratory therapy technician.section 1395x(r) of this title

children.heading: Grievance procedures
children.label: 4 Grievance procedures
children.text: Each medicaid managed care organization shall establish an internal grievance procedure under which an enrollee who is eligible for medical assistance under the State plan under this subchapter, or a provider on behalf of such an enrollee, may challenge the denial of coverage of or payment for such assistance.

children.heading: Demonstration of adequate capacity and services
children.label: 5 Demonstration of adequate capacity and services
A
children.text: offers an appropriate range of services and access to preventive and primary care services for the population expected to be enrolled in such service area, and

children.label: B
children.text: maintains a sufficient number, mix, and geographic distribution of providers of services.

children.heading: Protecting enrollees against liability for payment
children.label: 6 Protecting enrollees against liability for payment
A
children.text: for the debts of the organization, in the event of the organization’s insolvency,

children.label: i
children.text: in the event of the organization failing to receive payment from the State for such services; or

children.label: ii
children.text: in the event of a health care provider with a contractual, referral, or other arrangement with the organization failing to receive payment from the State or the organization for such services, or

children.label: C
children.text: for payments to a provider that furnishes covered services under a contractual, referral, or other arrangement with the organization in excess of the amount that would be owed by the individual if the organization had directly provided the services.

children.heading: Antidiscrimination
children.label: 7 Antidiscrimination
children.text: A medicaid managed care organization shall not discriminate with respect to participation, reimbursement, or indemnification as to any provider who is acting within the scope of the provider’s license or certification under applicable State law, solely on the basis of such license or certification. This paragraph shall not be construed to prohibit an organization from including providers only to the extent necessary to meet the needs of the organization’s enrollees or from establishing any measure designed to maintain quality and control costs consistent with the responsibilities of the organization.

children.heading: Compliance with certain maternity and mental health requirements
children.label: 8 Compliance with certain maternity and mental health requirements
children.text: Each medicaid managed care organization shall comply with the requirements of subpart 2 of part A of title XXVII of the Public Health Service Act insofar as such requirements apply and are effective with respect to a health insurance issuer that offers group health insurance coverage. In applying the previous sentence with respect to requirements under paragraph (8) of , a Medicaid managed care organization (or a prepaid inpatient health plan (as defined by the Secretary) or prepaid ambulatory health plan (as defined by the Secretary) that offers services to enrollees of a Medicaid managed care organization) shall be treated as in compliance with such requirements if the Medicaid managed care organization (or prepaid inpatient health plan or prepaid ambulatory health plan) is in compliance with subpart K of part 438 of title 42, Code of Federal Regulations, and section 438.3(n) of such title, or any successor regulation.1section 300gg–26(a) of this title

children.heading: Access standards
children.label: i Access standards
children.text: Standards for access to care so that covered services are available within reasonable timeframes and in a manner that ensures continuity of care and adequate primary care and specialized services capacity.

children.heading: Other measures
children.label: ii Other measures
children.text: Examination of other aspects of care and service directly related to the improvement of quality of care (including grievance procedures and marketing and information standards).

children.heading: Monitoring procedures
children.label: iii Monitoring procedures
children.text: Procedures for monitoring and evaluating the quality and appropriateness of care and services to enrollees that reflect the full spectrum of populations enrolled under the contract and that includes requirements for provision of quality assurance data to the State using the data and information set that the Secretary has specified for use under part C of subchapter XVIII or such alternative data as the Secretary approves, in consultation with the State.

children.heading: Periodic review
children.label: iv Periodic review
children.text: Regular, periodic examinations of the scope and content of the strategy.

children.heading: Standards
children.label: B Standards
children.text: The strategy developed under subparagraph (A) shall be consistent with standards that the Secretary first establishes within 1 year after . Such standards shall not preempt any State standards that are more stringent than such standards. Guidelines relating to quality assurance that are applied under shall apply under this subsection until the effective date of standards for quality assurance established under this subparagraph.August 5, 1997section 1396n(b)(1) of this title

children.heading: Monitoring
children.label: C Monitoring
children.text: The Secretary shall monitor the development and implementation of strategies under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: D Consultation
children.text: The Secretary shall conduct activities under subparagraphs (B) and (C) in consultation with the States.

children.heading: External independent review of managed care activities
children.label: 2 External independent review of managed care activities

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Each contract under with a medicaid managed care organization shall provide for an annual (as appropriate) external independent review conducted by a qualified independent entity of the quality outcomes and timeliness of, and access to, the items and services for which the organization is responsible under the contract. The requirement for such a review shall not apply until after the date that the Secretary establishes the identification method described in clause (ii).section 1396b(m) of this title

children.heading: Qualifications of reviewer
children.label: ii Qualifications of reviewer
children.text: The Secretary, in consultation with the States, shall establish a method for the identification of entities that are qualified to conduct reviews under clause (i).

children.heading: Use of protocols
children.label: iii Use of protocols
children.text: The Secretary, in coordination with the National Governors’ Association, shall contract with an independent quality review organization (such as the National Committee for Quality Assurance) to develop the protocols to be used in external independent reviews conducted under this paragraph on and after .January 1, 1999

children.heading: Availability of results
children.label: iv Availability of results
children.text: The results of each external independent review conducted under this subparagraph shall be available to participating health care providers, enrollees, and potential enrollees of the organization, except that the results may not be made available in a manner that discloses the identity of any individual patient.

children.heading: Nonduplication of accreditation
children.label: B Nonduplication of accreditation
children.text: A State may provide that, in the case of a medicaid managed care organization that is accredited by a private independent entity (such as those described in ) or that has an external review conducted under , the external review activities conducted under subparagraph (A) with respect to the organization shall not be duplicative of review activities conducted as part of the accreditation process or the external review conducted under such section.section 1395w–22(e)(4) of this titlesection 1395w–22(e)(3) of this title

children.heading: Deemed compliance for medicare managed care organizations
children.label: C Deemed compliance for medicare managed care organizations
children.text: At the option of a State, the requirements of subparagraph (A) shall not apply with respect to a medicaid managed care organization if the organization is an eligible organization with a contract in effect under or a Medicare+ÐChoice organization with a contract in effect under part C of subchapter XVIII and the organization has had a contract in effect under at least during the previous 2-year period.section 1395mm of this titlesection 1396b(m) of this title

children.heading: Protections against fraud and abuse
children.label: d Protections against fraud and abuse

children.heading: Prohibiting affiliations with individuals debarred by Federal agencies
children.label: 1 Prohibiting affiliations with individuals debarred by Federal agencies
i
children.text: have a person described in subparagraph (C) as a director, officer, partner, or person with beneficial ownership of more than 5 percent of the entity’s equity, or

children.label: ii
children.text: have an employment, consulting, or other agreement with a person described in such subparagraph for the provision of items and services that are significant and material to the entity’s obligations under its contract with the State.

children.label: i
children.text: shall notify the Secretary of such noncompliance;

children.label: ii
children.text: may continue an existing agreement with the entity unless the Secretary (in consultation with the Inspector General of the Department of Health and Human Services) directs otherwise; and

children.label: iii
children.text: may not renew or otherwise extend the duration of an existing agreement with the entity unless the Secretary (in consultation with the Inspector General of the Department of Health and Human Services) provides to the State and to Congress a written statement describing compelling reasons that exist for renewing or extending the agreement.

children.label: i
children.text: is debarred, suspended, or otherwise excluded from participating in procurement activities under the Federal Acquisition Regulation or from participating in nonprocurement activities under regulations issued pursuant to Executive Order No. 12549 or under guidelines implementing such order; or

children.label: ii
children.text: is an affiliate (as defined in such Regulation) of a person described in clause (i).

children.label: I
children.text: without the prior approval of the State, and

children.label: II
children.text: that contain false or materially misleading information.

children.heading: Consultation in review of market materials
children.label: ii Consultation in review of market materials
children.text: In the process of reviewing and approving such materials, the State shall provide for consultation with a medical care advisory committee.

children.heading: Service market
children.label: B Service market
children.text: A managed care entity shall distribute marketing materials to the entire service area of such entity covered under the contract under or .section 1396b(m) of this titlesection 1396d(t)(3) of this title

children.heading: Prohibition of tie-ins
children.label: C Prohibition of tie-ins
children.text: A managed care entity, or any agency of such entity, may not seek to influence an individual’s enrollment with the entity in conjunction with the sale of any other insurance.

children.heading: Prohibiting marketing fraud
children.label: D Prohibiting marketing fraud
children.text: Each managed care entity shall comply with such procedures and conditions as the Secretary prescribes in order to ensure that, before an individual is enrolled with the entity, the individual is provided accurate oral and written information sufficient to make an informed decision whether or not to enroll.

children.heading: Prohibition of “cold-call” marketing
children.label: E Prohibition of “cold-call” marketing
children.text: Each managed care entity shall not, directly or indirectly, conduct door-to-door, telephonic, or other “cold-call” marketing of enrollment under this subchapter.

children.heading: State conflict-of-interest safeguards in medicaid risk contracting
children.label: 3 State conflict-of-interest safeguards in medicaid risk contracting
children.text: A medicaid managed care organization may not enter into a contract with any State under unless the State has in effect conflict-of-interest safeguards with respect to officers and employees of the State with responsibilities relating to contracts with such organizations or to the default enrollment process described in subsection (a)(4)(C)(ii) that are at least as effective as the Federal safeguards provided under chapter 21 of title 41, against conflicts of interest that apply with respect to Federal procurement officials with comparable responsibilities with respect to such contracts.section 1396b(m) of this title

children.heading: Use of unique physician identifier for participating physicians
children.label: 4 Use of unique physician identifier for participating physicians
children.text: Each medicaid managed care organization shall require each physician providing services to enrollees eligible for medical assistance under the State plan under this subchapter to have a unique identifier in accordance with the system established under .section 1320d–2(b) of this title

children.heading: Contract requirement for managed care entities
children.label: 5 Contract requirement for managed care entities
children.text: With respect to any contract with a managed care entity under section 1396b(m) or 1396d(t)(3) of this title (as applicable), no later than , such contract shall include a provision that providers of services or persons terminated (as described in ) from participation under this subchapter, subchapter XVIII, or subchapter XXI shall be terminated from participating under this subchapter as a provider in any network of such entity that serves individuals eligible to receive medical assistance under this subchapter.July 1, 2018section 1396a(kk)(8) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Beginning not later than , a State shall require that, in order to participate as a provider in the network of a managed care entity that provides services to, or orders, prescribes, refers, or certifies eligibility for services for, individuals who are eligible for medical assistance under the State plan under this subchapter (or under a waiver of the plan) and who are enrolled with the entity, the provider is enrolled consistent with with the State agency administering the State plan under this subchapter. Such enrollment shall include providing to the State agency the provider’s identifying information, including the name, specialty, date of birth, Social Security number, national provider identifier, Federal taxpayer identification number, and the State license or certification number of the provider.January 1, 2018section 1396a(kk) of this title

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: B Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in subparagraph (A) shall be construed as requiring a provider described in such subparagraph to provide services to individuals who are not enrolled with a managed care entity under this subchapter.

children.heading: Use of intermediate sanctions by the State to enforce requirements
children.label: 1 Use of intermediate sanctions by the State to enforce requirements
i
children.text: fails substantially to provide medically necessary items and services that are required (under law or under such organization’s contract with the State) to be provided to an enrollee covered under the contract;

children.label: ii
children.text: imposes premiums or charges on enrollees in excess of the premiums or charges permitted under this subchapter;

children.label: iii
children.text: acts to discriminate among enrollees on the basis of their health status or requirements for health care services, including expulsion or refusal to reenroll an individual, except as permitted by this subchapter, or engaging in any practice that would reasonably be expected to have the effect of denying or discouraging enrollment with the organization by eligible individuals whose medical condition or history indicates a need for substantial future medical services;

children.label: I
children.text: to the Secretary or the State under this subchapter; or

children.label: II
children.text: to an enrollee, potential enrollee, or a health care provider under such subchapter; or

children.label: v
children.text: fails to comply with the applicable requirements of .section 1396b(m)(2)(A)(x) of this title

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: B Rule of construction
children.text: Clause (i) of subparagraph (A) shall not apply to the provision of abortion services, except that a State may impose a sanction on any medicaid managed care organization that has a contract to provide abortion services if the organization does not provide such services as provided for under the contract.

children.label: i
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), (iii), or (iv), not more than $25,000 for each determination under paragraph (1)(A).

children.label: ii
children.text: With respect to a determination under clause (iii) or (iv)(I) of paragraph (1)(A), not more than $100,000 for each such determination.

children.label: iii
children.text: With respect to a determination under paragraph (1)(A)(ii), double the excess amount charged in violation of such subsection (and the excess amount charged shall be deducted from the penalty and returned to the individual concerned).

children.label: iv
children.text: Subject to clause (ii), with respect to a determination under paragraph (1)(A)(iii), $15,000 for each individual not enrolled as a result of a practice described in such subsection.

children.label: i
children.text: to oversee the operation of the medicaid managed care organization upon a finding by the State that there is continued egregious behavior by the organization or there is a substantial risk to the health of enrollees; or

children.label: I
children.text: there is an orderly termination or reorganization of the organization; or

children.label: II
children.text: improvements are made to remedy the violations found under paragraph (1),

children.label: C
children.text: Permitting individuals enrolled with the managed care entity to terminate enrollment without cause, and notifying such individuals of such right to terminate enrollment.

children.label: D
children.text: Suspension or default of all enrollment of individuals under this subchapter after the date the Secretary or the State notifies the entity of a determination of a violation of any requirement of or this section.section 1396b(m) of this title

children.label: E
children.text: Suspension of payment to the entity under this subchapter for individuals enrolled after the date the Secretary or State notifies the entity of such a determination and until the Secretary or State is satisfied that the basis for such determination has been corrected and is not likely to recur.

children.heading: Treatment of chronic substandard entities
children.label: 3 Treatment of chronic substandard entities
children.text: In the case of a medicaid managed care organization which has repeatedly failed to meet the requirements of and this section, the State shall (regardless of what other sanctions are provided) impose the sanctions described in subparagraphs (B) and (C) of paragraph (2).section 1396b(m) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of a managed care entity which has failed to meet the requirements of this part or a contract under section 1396b(m) or 1396d(t)(3) of this title, the State shall have the authority to terminate such contract with the entity and to enroll such entity’s enrollees with other managed care entities (or to permit such enrollees to receive medical assistance under the State plan under this subchapter other than through a managed care entity).

children.heading: Availability of hearing prior to termination of contract
children.label: B Availability of hearing prior to termination of contract
children.text: A State may not terminate a contract with a managed care entity under subparagraph (A) unless the entity is provided with a hearing prior to the termination.

children.heading: Notice and right to disenroll in cases of termination hearing
children.label: C Notice and right to disenroll in cases of termination hearing
i
children.text: notify individuals enrolled with a managed care entity which is the subject of a hearing to terminate the entity’s contract with the State of the hearing, and

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of such an entity, permit such enrollees to disenroll immediately with the entity without cause.

children.heading: Other protections for managed care entities against sanctions imposed by State
children.label: 5 Other protections for managed care entities against sanctions imposed by State
children.text: Before imposing any sanction against a managed care entity other than termination of the entity’s contract, the State shall provide the entity with notice and such other due process protections as the State may provide, except that a State may not provide a managed care entity with a pre-termination hearing before imposing the sanction described in paragraph (2)(B).

children.heading: Timeliness of payment; adequacy of payment for primary care services
children.label: f Timeliness of payment; adequacy of payment for primary care services
children.text: A contract under with a medicaid managed care organization shall provide that the organization shall make payment to health care providers for items and services which are subject to the contract and that are furnished to individuals eligible for medical assistance under the State plan under this subchapter who are enrolled with the organization on a timely basis consistent with the claims payment procedures described in , unless the health care provider and the organization agree to an alternate payment schedule and, in the case of primary care services described in , consistent with the minimum payment rates specified in such section (regardless of the manner in which such payments are made, including in the form of capitation or partial capitation).section 1396b(m) of this titlesection 1396a(a)(37)(A) of this titlesection 1396a(a)(13)(C) of this title

children.heading: Identification of patients for purposes of making DSH payments
children.label: g Identification of patients for purposes of making DSH payments

children.text: to report to the State information necessary to determine the hospital services provided under the contract (and the identity of hospitals providing such services) for purposes of applying sections 1395ww(d)(5)(F) and 1396r–4 of this title; or

to include a sponsorship code in the identification card issued to individuals covered under this subchapter in order that a hospital may identify a patient as being entitled to benefits under this subchapter.

children.heading: Special rules with respect to Indian enrollees, Indian health care providers, and Indian managed care entities
children.label: h Special rules with respect to Indian enrollees, Indian health care providers, and Indian managed care entities

children.heading: Enrollee option to select an Indian health care provider as primary care provider
children.label: 1 Enrollee option to select an Indian health care provider as primary care provider
A
children.text: has an Indian enrolled with the entity; and

children.label: B
children.text: has an Indian health care provider that is participating as a primary care provider within the network of the entity,

children.heading: Assurance of payment to Indian health care providers for provision of covered services
children.label: 2 Assurance of payment to Indian health care providers for provision of covered services

children.heading: Demonstration of access to Indian health care providers and application of alternative payment arrangements
children.label: A Demonstration of access to Indian health care providers and application of alternative payment arrangements
i
children.text: demonstrate that the number of Indian health care providers that are participating providers with respect to such entity are sufficient to ensure timely access to covered Medicaid managed care services for those Indian enrollees who are eligible to receive services from such providers; and

children.label: ii
children.text: agree to pay Indian health care providers, whether such providers are participating or nonparticipating providers with respect to the entity, for covered Medicaid managed care services provided to those Indian enrollees who are eligible to receive services from such providers at a rate equal to the rate negotiated between such entity and the provider involved or, if such a rate has not been negotiated, at a rate that is not less than the level and amount of payment which the entity would make for the services if the services were furnished by a participating provider which is not an Indian health care provider.

children.heading: Prompt payment
children.label: B Prompt payment
children.text: To agree to make prompt payment (consistent with rule for prompt payment of providers under ) to Indian health care providers that are participating providers with respect to such entity or, in the case of an entity to which subparagraph (A)(ii) or (C) applies, that the entity is required to pay in accordance with that subparagraph.section 1396u–2(f) of this title

children.heading: Application of special payment requirements for federally-qualified health centers and for services provided by certain Indian health care providers
children.label: C Application of special payment requirements for federally-qualified health centers and for services provided by certain Indian health care providers

children.heading: Managed care entity payment requirement
children.label: I Managed care entity payment requirement
children.text: To agree to pay any Indian health care provider that is a federally-qualified health center under this subchapter but not a participating provider with respect to the entity, for the provision of covered Medicaid managed care services by such provider to an Indian enrollee of the entity at a rate equal to the amount of payment that the entity would pay a federally-qualified health center that is a participating provider with respect to the entity but is not an Indian health care provider for such services.

children.heading: Continued application of State requirement to make supplemental payment
children.label: II Continued application of State requirement to make supplemental payment
children.text: Nothing in subclause (I) or subparagraph (A) or (B) shall be construed as waiving the application of regarding the State plan requirement to make any supplemental payment due under such section to a federally-qualified health center for services furnished by such center to an enrollee of a managed care entity (regardless of whether the federally-qualified health center is or is not a participating provider with the entity).section 1396a(bb)(5) of this title

children.heading: Payment rate for services provided by certain Indian health care providers
children.label: ii Payment rate for services provided by certain Indian health care providers
children.text: If the amount paid by a managed care entity to an Indian health care provider that is not a federally-qualified health center for services provided by the provider to an Indian enrollee with the managed care entity is less than the rate that applies to the provision of such services by the provider under the State plan, the plan shall provide for payment to the Indian health care provider, whether the provider is a participating or nonparticipating provider with respect to the entity, of the difference between such applicable rate and the amount paid by the managed care entity to the provider for such services.

children.heading: Construction
children.label: D Construction
children.text: Nothing in this paragraph shall be construed as waiving the application of (relating to application of standards to assure that payments are consistent with efficiency, economy, and quality of care).section 1396a(a)(30)(A) of this title

children.heading: Special rule for enrollment for Indian managed care entities
children.label: 3 Special rule for enrollment for Indian managed care entities
children.text: Regarding the application of a Medicaid managed care program to Indian Medicaid managed care entities, an Indian Medicaid managed care entity may restrict enrollment under such program to Indians in the same manner as Indian Health Programs may restrict the delivery of services to Indians.

children.heading: Indian health care provider
children.label: A Indian health care provider
children.text: The term “Indian health care provider” means an Indian Health Program or an Urban Indian Organization.

children.heading: Indian Medicaid managed care entity
children.label: B Indian Medicaid managed care entity
children.text: The term “Indian Medicaid managed care entity” means a managed care entity that is controlled (within the meaning of the last sentence of ) by the Indian Health Service, a Tribe, Tribal Organization, or Urban Indian Organization, or a consortium, which may be composed of 1 or more Tribes, Tribal Organizations, or Urban Indian Organizations, and which also may include the Service.section 1396b(m)(1)(C) of this title

children.heading: Non-Indian Medicaid managed care entity
children.label: C Non-Indian Medicaid managed care entity
children.text: The term “non-Indian Medicaid managed care entity” means a managed care entity that is not an Indian Medicaid managed care entity.

children.heading: Covered Medicaid managed care services
children.label: D Covered Medicaid managed care services
children.text: The term “covered Medicaid managed care services” means, with respect to an individual enrolled with a managed care entity, items and services for which benefits are available with respect to the individual under the contract between the entity and the State involved.

children.heading: Medicaid managed care program
children.label: E Medicaid managed care program
children.text: The term “Medicaid managed care program” means a program under sections 1396b(m), 1396d(t), and 1396u–2 of this title and includes a managed care program operating under a waiver under section 1396n(b) or 1315 of this title or otherwise.

children.heading: Drug utilization review activities and requirements
children.label: i Drug utilization review activities and requirements
children.text: Beginning not later than , each contract under a State plan with a managed care entity (other than a primary care case manager) under shall provide that the entity is in compliance with the applicable provisions of section 438.3(s)(2) of title 42, Code of Federal Regulations, section 483.3(s)(4)) of such title, and section 483.3(s)(5) of such title, as such provisions were in effect on .October 1, 2019March 31, 2018section 1396b(m) of this title23So in original. Probably should be section “438.3(s)(4)” and second parenthesis probably should not appear.2So in original. Probably should be section “438.3(s)(5)”.3

children.heading: Transmission of address information
children.label: j Transmission of address information
children.text: Beginning , each contract under a State plan with a managed care entity (as defined in ) or with a prepaid inpatient health plan or prepaid ambulatory health plan (as such terms are defined in ), shall provide that such entity or plan shall promptly transmit to the State any address information for an individual enrolled with such entity or plan that is provided to such entity or plan directly from, or verified by such entity or plan directly with, such individual.January 1, 2027section 1396u–2(a)(1)(B) of this titlesection 1396b(m)(9)(D) of this title
